summary: Senior-year prep is heating up: lock the JHU APL mentorship application, keep SAT momentum, and reply to two time-sensitive emails.

sections.title: Top Things I Need To Know Today

items.title: JHU APL ASPIRE mentorship application is the single highest-leverage thing this week
items.body: Local AI/CS research mentorship that fits your college story almost perfectly. The application window is open now and fills fast.

items.title: Two emails need a reply today
items.body: A counselor email about senior schedule changes and a professor's reply about a possible summer research read-along.

items.title: SAT is 9 weeks out — you're on pace if you keep 2 sessions/week
items.body: Math is solid; reading pace is the limiter. Today's focus: one timed reading section.

items.title: Confirm driving test appointment
items.body: MVA road test is in two weeks. Confirm the slot and make sure logged practice hours are complete.

items.title: Diksuchi session plan due to the team
items.body: You volunteered to lead next week's session — share the outline so others can prep.

items.title: Atmiya Code Club: pick the summer mini-project
items.body: Decide between the beginner Python track and a small web app so you can announce it Friday.

items.title: Senior schedule change — action needed
items.summary: Counselor proposes swapping a study hall for AP CS A or Data Science. Reply with your preference by Thursday.

items.title: Re: Summer reading + possible research help
items.summary: A professor replied — open to a short reading project on ML fairness if you can commit ~3 hrs/week.

items.title: Diksuchi volunteer hours confirmation
items.summary: Your logged hours for May were approved; June log is open for entries.

items.title: Congressional App Challenge registration is open
items.summary: MD District 3 registration opened. Solo or team submissions accepted through the fall.

items.title: JHU APL ASPIRE — High School STEM Mentorship

metadata.label: golden
metadata.fitReason: Year-long, local AI/CS research mentorship with a real engineer — exactly the kind of sustained, credible project that strengthens a CS-focused application.

metadata.label: strong
metadata.fitReason: Funds and mentors an original STEM project you propose — pairs well with a CS/AI build you can start this summer.

metadata.label: strong
metadata.fitReason: Local, beginner-friendly, and recognized by a member of Congress — a clean win you can ship solo or with Atmiya Code Club.

metadata.label: maybe
metadata.fitReason: Fun and local, but overlaps with SAT prep — only worth it if the weekend stays open.

metadata.label: ignore
metadata.fitReason: Over the $2,500 cap and offers little a free, project-based path wouldn't. Skip.

items.title: Daily report delivered successfully
items.body: Today's 9:00 AM report was generated and pushed to your dashboard.

items.title: Reminder: rotate the ingest secret monthly
items.body: Good hygiene for the push endpoint. Update REPORT_INGEST_SECRET in Vercel and your ChatGPT task together.

items.title: New open-weight small models keep getting better at coding
items.summary: Worth trying one for an offline coding helper on your laptop — a good portfolio note if you benchmark it.

items.title: Free MIT/Stanford intro-to-ML materials updated for 2026
items.summary: Refreshed notebooks make a solid self-study spine if you take on the THINK project.

items.title: Maryland expands dual-enrollment options for the coming school year
items.summary: More community-college CS courses may count for high-school credit — ask your counselor if it fits senior year.

items.title: College Board confirms fall SAT dates and registration windows
items.summary: Register early for your preferred local test center before it fills.

metadata.whyItMatters: Getting your license frees up time and looks good for independence on applications.
metadata.nextStep: Confirm the appointment and finish required practice hours.

metadata.whyItMatters: Your golden opportunity — missing this closes the door for the year.
metadata.nextStep: Draft the short essay this week while it's fresh.

metadata.whyItMatters: Sets the cadence for applications, courses, and leadership roles.

metadata.nextStep: Hold two prep sessions per week; prioritize timed reading.
